rationale: (1) __testing seam: 현재 nodes.ts 끝단(L519 이후)에는 __testing export 가 없음 → SOL 이 이를 fix 와 함께 신규 추가한다고 명시 (솔루션 본문 L193-207). 동일 경로 sibling agent-wait-dedupe.ts:223 / agents.ts:78 / usage.ts:355 에 __testing 또는 __test 패턴이 실존 확인 → 패턴 정합. drafted test 의 import `{ __testing as wakeTesting, maybeWakeNodeWithApns }` 는 seam 추가 직후 resolve 됨. (2) mocks.loadApnsRegistration.mockResolvedValue(null) 은 production hot-path 와 byte-identical: nodes.ts L333 `const registration = await loadApnsRegistration(nodeId); if (!registration) return withDuration({ path: 'no-registration' })`. 실제 미등록 nodeId 가 들어오면 infra/push-apns.js 의 loadApnsRegistration 도 정확히 null 반환 → production 에서 taken 되는 같은 branch. 이미 nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts L279/491/572/640 이 같은 패턴으로 branch 를 exercise 중 (동일 vi.mock target `../../infra/push-apns.js` 확인). CAL-003 의 process.kill throw 같은 강제 분기 없음. (3) maybeWakeNodeWithApns 의 signature `(nodeId: string, opts?: {force?:boolean; wakeReason?:string})` — 실제 caller (nodes.ts:920 `maybeWakeNodeWithApns(nodeId)`, L943 `(nodeId, {force:true})`, L1050 `(nodeId)`, nodes-pending.ts:89 `(p.nodeId, {wakeReason:'node.pending'})`) 와 일치. drafted test 는 `maybeWakeNodeWithApns(`unregistered-node-${i}`)` — nodeId 만 전달하는 L920/L1050 caller 패턴 exactly. (4) Pre-fix FAIL / post-fix PASS differentiation: 현재 main 의 L312-313 unconditional set + L334-336 early return (delete 없음) → 50 개 unregistered nodeId 호출 후 nodeWakeById.size === 50 → `expect(getNodeWakeByIdSize()).toBe(0)` 실패. Fix (L335 직전 nodeWakeById.delete(nodeId)) 적용 시 → size === 0 pass. 결정적 differentiation. state.inFlight 의 finally nullify (L413-415) 확인됨 → entry 자체의 size 가 deterministically 0 또는 N. 타이머 / async race 의존성 없음 (각 await 완료 후 측정). (5) CAL-003 parallel: differs. CAL-003 (CAND-006) 은 test 가 process.kill throw 경로를 mock 으로 강제했으나 production 은 process.emit 경로만 탐 → hot-path 불일치. 본 SOL 은 production 에서 미등록 nodeId 가 들어올 때 loadApnsRegistration 이 실제로 null 반환 → test 가 강제하는 상태가 production 에서 발생 가능. operator RPC (node.pending.enqueue, node.invoke) 가 arbitrary nodeId 를 허용하는 reachability 도 FIND-002 mechanism 에 기술 + validator 부재도 grep 으로 확인 가능. 추가 관찰: 초안에서 "getNodeWakeByIdSize" 키가 resetWakeState/hasNodeWakeEntry 와 동시에 정의되어야 테스트가 resolve — SOL 본문 (L193-207) 에서 세 함수 export 명시 확인. 유일 잔여 리스크: (a) 50 iteration 이 다소 과잉 (1 iteration + single assertion 으로도 증명 가능; 2nd it block 이 이미 minimal case) — 테스트 가독성/확신 보강 수단으로 수용 가능. (b) `nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts` 와 신규 파일 간 module-level nodeWakeById 상태 간섭 가능성 — vitest 가 파일 단위 격리 (default pool) 이고 각 테스트의 beforeEach 가 resetWakeState 로 clear 하므로 무해. Net: hot-path 일치 + 결정적 + seam 근거 + caller signature 일치 → proceed.

Direct verification in openclaw tree confirms every load-bearing claim: (a) nodes.ts:312-313 does unconditional nodeWakeById.set before L333 loadApnsRegistration; (b) L334-336 no-registration branch returns without any delete; (c) the ONLY delete path is clearNodeWakeState (L525-528) wired solely from ws-connection.ts:327 under triple condition (role=node + unregister returns truthy nodeId + WS close fired); (d) PR #63709 commit 36c3a54b51 (Menglin Li, 2026-04-11 per git log — one day drift from SOL's 2026-04-09) introduced clearNodeWakeState + the single WS-close call site, but DID NOT touch the no-registration early-return branch — the gap is genuine. chosen_fix=0 (cleanup-on-early-return, 3-line body diff) is the minimal-scope primary fix: it targets the exact leak path, preserves the state.inFlight coalescing invariant (promise refs are held by awaiters directly, so deleting the map entry does not cancel in-flight work — consistent with production code at L411-415 which already nullifies state.inFlight in finally). Candidate 1 (reorder-check-before-set) is correctly rejected: the FIND root_cause_chain[0] identifies the set-before-check as intentional for concurrent coalescing, and reorder would duplicate loadApnsRegistration I/O under concurrent calls. Candidate 2 (guard-with-hasRegistration) is correctly rejected: push-apns.ts has no cheap has() API, would require an adjacent-module API addition (violates one-thing-per-PR). The __testing seam is realistic scope — the exact pattern (export const __testing) already exists at agent-wait-dedupe.ts:223 and agents.ts:78 within the same folder, so the seam addition matches established convention rather than introducing a novel observability mechanism. The seam is necessary because nodeWakeById is module-private and the leak symptom ({ lastWakeAtMs: 0 } zombies) does not affect throttle return shape — behavior-only assertions cannot observe it. The existing nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts has 4 no-registration-branch tests (L279/491/572/640) that only assert return shape, so adding delete will not break them. PR #63709 scope delineation table is concrete and accurate: the PR addressed registered-lifecycle cleanup (WS close path), this SOL extends to WS-never-completed path — distinct code paths, non-overlapping coverage. The cover-letter framing 'extends the same helper coverage to a different code path' is maintainer-receptive because it (i) explicitly references the prior PR by number + commit SHA, (ii) cites the exact gap, (iii) matches openclaw maintainer focus (memory/reliability per CLAUDE.md). change_scope=XS is realistic: ~3-line body + ~12-line __testing block + new test file, well under the 50-line XS threshold. Minor drift: SOL says #63709 merged 2026-04-09 but git shows commit date 2026-04-11 — cosmetic, doesn't affect correctness.

Attacked the 4 devil's-advocate angles and found the drafter's case holds on substance, though seam ergonomics carry residual review risk. (1) reorder-check-before-set is NOT better: nodes.ts:312-325 shows set(L313) + state.inFlight assignment(L325) + concurrent caller's `await state.inFlight` via get()(L312) share the SAME state reference — this is the coalescing invariant. Moving set after loadApnsRegistration splits the state identity between concurrent callers, causing duplicate loadApnsRegistration I/O. FIND-002 root_cause_chain[0] correctly identifies this. Candidate 0 is the minimal-delta that preserves the invariant while closing the leak. (2) __testing seam is NOT CAL-001 defense-in-depth reprise: CAL-001 was a secondary production mechanism (sweeper keep-alive) added atop an existing primary cleanup; here no primary cleanup exists for the no-registration path and the seam is purely observability with zero production behavior. Grep confirms 7+ established sibling patterns: agent-wait-dedupe.ts:223, agents.ts:78, usage.ts:355, control-plane-rate-limit.ts:112, call.ts:156, server-startup-post-attach.ts:362, openresponses-http.ts:200 — this is house style, not scope-creep. (3) PR #63709 scope-confusion close-risk: LOW. The drafter could reuse clearNodeWakeState(nodeId) helper (same API as #63709) instead of raw nodeWakeById.delete(nodeId) to explicitly frame this as 'extending the existing helper to one more call site' — this actually makes the PR narrative CLEANER and harder to close as duplicate. Recommendation: consider calling clearNodeWakeState(nodeId) in the no-registration branch instead of inlining delete, to share the helper with #63709 and simplify the scope-delineation narrative. (4) state.inFlight promise ref: already resolved in the SOL's Open Question §4 via L413-415 finally nullify — not in scope, correctly excluded. Residual risks that keep confidence at medium rather than high: (a) a strict maintainer may ask 'why add __testing when nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts already imports clearNodeWakeState directly for other tests' — the behavioral answer (lastWakeAtMs=0 entries are invisible to throttle path L321 which requires `> 0`) is airtight but requires PR-body articulation; (b) helper-reuse nuance in point 3 is a narrative refinement not called out in the current chosen_fix diff.
